text.title: Match Real World Procedure
text.summary: Step-by-step procedure to apply the match-real-world methodology end-to-end.

step.name: extract-strings
step.action: Run templates/extract-strings.py (or grep) to pull all user-visible strings with identifiers.

step.name: categorise
step.action: Tag each candidate with category: jargon | inconsistency | locale-blind | over-simplified-domain-term | icon-only | system-centric.
step.decision-gate: If audience is domain experts and category is over-simplified-domain-term, skip-this-methodology for that string.

step.name: cite-user-evidence
step.action: For each candidate find at least one citation in the user-vocabulary corpus (ticket id / transcript line / search-log query). Candidates without citation are dropped.

step.name: propose-rewrite
step.action: Draft a rewrite grounded in the cited user vocabulary.

step.name: consistency-pass
step.action: Cross-check whether each rewrite needs propagation across web / mobile / email / docs; set `consistency_check`.
step.decision-gate: If category=inconsistency and consistency_check=passes, block; this is a contradiction.
